name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-104
title: Hook command paths in settings.json are relative; fragile under CWD changes from Bash cd
description:
All four event-dispatcher hooks plus scope-guard.py are registered in .claude/settings.json with RELATIVE paths:

  'command': '.claude/hooks/scope-guard.py'
  'command': '.claude/hooks/stop-dispatcher.py'
  'command': '.claude/hooks/session-start-dispatcher.py'
  'command': '.claude/hooks/user-prompt-submit-dispatcher.py'

These paths resolve against the session's current working directory at hook-fire time. Claude Code's Bash tool persists CWD between commands. Any cd into a subdirectory makes the relative path resolve outside the repo and the hook errors with '/bin/sh: .claude/hooks/<name>.py: No such file or directory'.

Claude Code marks the error 'non-blocking' so the user's Bash call proceeds, but the noise pollutes session output and any user action (drift detection, scope-guard checks, welcome banner, prompt counter) that the hook was supposed to perform silently does not happen for that Bash call.

REPRODUCTION (HEAD 1d10c86b, ws04 wave session):
  Bash 1: cd .claude/features/contract && python3 test/run.py  → CWD now .claude/features/contract
  Bash 2: cd .claude/features/rabbit-cage && python3 test/run.py
    → 'cd' fails: no such file or directory (relative cd from contract dir)
    → PreToolUse hook fires with relative '.claude/hooks/scope-guard.py' resolved against contract dir → file not found → 'Failed with non-blocking status code'
  Bash 3 (with absolute path workaround): cd /abs/path/ws04 && python3 .claude/features/rabbit-cage/test/run.py
    → Still triggers the hook from prior CWD (.claude/features/contract) before the cd takes effect → same hook error

IMPACT:
- Cosmetic noise on every Bash call after a session's first cd
- Silent loss of drift/scope-guard/banner work for those calls (non-blocking means the work is skipped, not retried)
- Subagents that operate in subdirectories (common pattern) trigger this on every Bash tool call

PROPOSED FIX:
Use a repo-root-anchored path. Two options:

1. Absolute path resolved at install time — install.py writes the absolute repo path into settings.json's hook commands. Downside: settings.json becomes machine-specific and cannot be committed (currently it IS committed). Would need to gitignore settings.json or split into committed+local.

2. Claude Code environment variable — if Claude Code exposes a CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR or similar env var pointing to the repo root, use it in the command: 'command': '/.claude/hooks/scope-guard.py'. Requires verifying Claude Code supports env var interpolation in hook commands.

3. Wrapper script — write a small bash wrapper that resolves '.claude/hooks/...' relative to git rev-parse --show-toplevel, install that as the hook command. Adds an indirection but keeps settings.json portable.

OWNER: rabbit-cage owns the four hook publish_hook manifest entries that write to settings.json. The fix involves publish_hook (in contract.lib.publish) understanding how to emit a repo-root-anchored path AND the source dispatchers being callable from any CWD.

DETECTED BY: ws04 session during CONTRACT-BACKLOG-38 wave (cross-feature scrub work), 2026-05-25.
